key_insight: A microstructure-conditioned hybrid surrogate, implemented with a hypernetwork, can accurately predict multiscale behavior across varying microstructures using far less data than standard surrogates.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical multiscale optimization of graded sustainable composites and supports microstructure-aware design that can significantly reduce peak stress.
method: Condition a physics-data hybrid surrogate on microstructural variables via a hypernetwork.
Use the surrogate inside multiscale optimization for functionally graded mycelium-woodchip composites.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables to link process inputs to macroscale response.
evidence_signal: The conditioned model was validated against full FE^2 simulation and used to optimize a graded disk, reducing peak stress by 42% relative to a random-microstructure design.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training data spans the relevant microstructure and manufacturing-variable space, so extrapolation outside the trained regime could be unreliable.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
